5-21-26.content:
hi
things i want to do for this website:

remove these journal entries from search
finish making sp block thing
styling! maybe draw some things or at least make it a lot more personal

long term:

make writing easier (can i write markdown from my phone/tablet??)
make that keyboard thing (ilo nena)/some way of inputting sp

thoughts of today:

the way beings adapt and exist in the real world is because they are reactionary thats how
they can find solutions to situations that aren’t completely preplanned. i think about this a
lot every time i ride a bike or throw something like i’m not a humanoid robot with these actions
fully programmed, we constantly observe and update and predict and tweak. like consistent
processed input leads to much better output. this comes from some sports yt video i watched
about throwing and also angela colliers video about humanoid robots and also robo kin and also
me being continually surprised at being able to bike no handed.
human input is so analog. i was thinking about why typing on keyboards feels so much worse than
writing characters by hand like its faster and more precise why is it worse. like typing linear
text might be better but thats literally it. i was thinking about a better way to navigate file
systems/trees and why double clicking files and folders still feels better than cd ls cd ls.

like ok sure it would be better navigation wise if it ls-ed every time you cd-ed, but having
to type the name of the dir/file every time you want to cd or open sucks so much butt. reading
is way faster than writing. i thought that maybe a system where you can like use vim commands
to cycle through files would be nicer than using the mouse but :Sex and :Vex is still ass. the
problem is that you have so little control over the speed of selection changing. like with a
mouse you can see that your folder is far away and start moving the mouse in that direction
fast, micro adjusting as you go along to hit the file. spamming jjjjjj may take the same time
but feels so much worse. even holding j down sucks bc the refresh rate of selection changing
is preset. the problem is if its too fast then it feels too slippery for small adjustments
(picking a file that is right next to the current selection) but if its slow its painful
watching it inch over when you already know where the file you want to end up on is. then i
realized that its because each key input is digital, on or off, theres no way to push the
button harder and get a longer jump. ok i just realized you kind of can the key presses can
have some sort of acceleration and it can even be like jumping in a video game except that has
predictable gravity which makes it so much more precision controllable to humans i think. also
the other problem is that only having 4 directions of movement control is so ass. ok but then
i thought about having analog pressure buttons which maybe were like moving and turning and then
i thought about trying to draw on a screen with these types of buttons and it would just be like
a horrible etch a sketch this idea needs more work.


see this is the problem it takes me so long to write these trains of thought down i literally
thought all of that in like 5 minutes in cs class as he was talking about file systems in class.
im so insecure w friends i want to like me like ofc i am but its so annoying bc i know how
pathetic i look and i hate HATE when other ppl are like that to me like grow a spine get some self
respect actually care about yourself more than me so why cant i do that with others

more abt dolmenwood
mothership/ito crossover horror elements
ways to have this cross-death knight system from mythic bastionland in an into the odd setting

maybe death isnt death???

what i want in an rpg:

non superheroic, non folk heroic, just heroic (in the sense that i want people to have character
arcs, be somewhat special, be not super in danger of dying, but be very in danger of messing up)
less, fast combat, no to hit, damage, damage, done

no waiting around → lazy dm said an interesting thing, weave narrative + exploration into combat


reward players coming up with ideas to solve things
inventory management i think ive come around on this resource management is cool and inventory
is cooler than character sheet/spell slot management → also more of a communal problem
not as much dungeon crawling as mystery solving

i love mysteries, horror, heists, political things


so maybe i dont like random encounters as much? ill have to think
combat should be a puzzle not just whack whack whack die

ig osr solves this by you actually just dying if you try to whack whack whack

this list needs more thought

colonize.title: colonize
colonize.content:
car said something that made me kind of autistically contemplate yesterday. I was telling her about how the word “bruzz”
is actually from my sister (I use this word a lot) and she said “💀 you colonized it”
thats like rly interesting paralleling linguistic/semantic space to real space like it redescribes the process of word
creation as like discovery instead of creation. i guess one way to think about it is semantic space is the natural world
and creating words is like defining borders on that world creating “states”.
theres also some sense of ownership of and influence over words with the colonizing description like I have influence over
all the borders of the words I use (i have a share of the word company 💀) which I guess you do and since I am one of
the only people in carissa’s life who uses the word bruzz, to her i essentially have complete dominion of the word “bruzz”.
me claiming that there was a point of time in the past where my sister had control over this word and i had none (and that
i had not even discovered it), along with how much i have exerted my influence on the word’s semantic borders and derivatives
(derivations?) → “colonize” makes a lot of sense
wow words are social thats crazy
what is a word
ok toki pona also kind of thinks of itself like this. except that theyre like lowk all socialists and had a sort of linguistic
revolution to redistribute control of the toki pona words’ semantic borders back to “the people”
ok this analogy kind of fails since words have overlapping borders and also what is the space of all possible words that is like
an insane thought (and by that i mean the space of all semantic meanings not literally the set of all words defined by a languages
phonemic inventory + phonological grammar)
i should stop talking.

game.title: game
game.content:
ive been into ttrpgs ever since my dad got me and my cousin to play dnd one boring week over at my grandmas. this was probably 2014.
since then ive played 4-5 campaigns and even ran my own which lasted about a year .. its really fun! i love dming and seeing my friends laugh and do bullshit and care about the lore
or not care and just exist in the world to dismember and torture sewer cultists. there arent many other places in life where you collaborate creatively i wonder why.
i keep coming back to this idea of osr games specifically the into the odd series of games. the mechanics are so elegant and there is something special about emphasizing creativity
in a less restrictive system than trying to assign stats and rules to everything. we have video games now this is not that. when i was a player of dnd my favorite part was looking
through new modules and class lists and making character after character excited about what different powers i could have or ways i could play the game. actually playing dnd was often
a slog, repeating the one cool thing my build optimized for and shoving the bard up front every time there was a social situation.
im drawn to the idea of everyone being able to fill any role they want at any time, but also character development needs to be able to happen. this is a balance that is ultimately up
to the dm i guess. players also need solid things in the world they can point to and work off of. a game with zero direction is not a successful one either. maybe an 80:20 dm/player
creative control balance is good?
i guess part of progression is being able to point to somewhere written down and say im good at a thing. and many of the “creative” solutions people come up with become boring the
second or third time theyre reused. either the dm should continually face the players with novel situations (which is extremely hard), or powers should come with limitations. a player
should never say “i’m just going to do the thing again” and go through the motions, they should have a goal, a way of getting there and a slight unique barrier they have to come up
with an idea to solve. this barrier also cant be so big as to make only the wildest solutions work, the game must be realistic (e.g. your leg is amputated > lets go look for a
prosthetics shop > *rolls* oh theres one in town > i pay money > great now youre the same as before) ← this is uninteresting!
i guess this is why i want to add more the mythic bastionland rules to ItO combat, people love combat, the added dice and tactics make it interesting! I don’t know if people will
find a way to abuse the same bonuses every time though → maybe thats a signal that the dm needs to react and have the enemies come up with a new way around that… thoughts.

index.title: A Box of Musings
index.content:
i am currently working on this, while i do you should learn toki pona! @tokipona.net


This is the only outward facing mu on this website. Anything else you find that directly/indirectly references a reader does not assume that YOU will be the one reading it.

poki mu la mu mi li lon.
mi kama jo e toki lili anu pilin la mi o pana e ona tawa lipu ni. kama la lipu ni li jo e mu mute mi.
taso tenpo wan la mi pali e lipu ni. ken la tenpo mu li kama lon weka pi tenpo seli ni.
This is poki mu. This roughly translates to “container of animal noises”. It is a void I will throw text into. It is also I guess my personal website.
I think people who intend their personal website to be useful to others are extremely self-centered. This website is for me and (occasionally) some of my friends
it’s not designed in a way that will make sense to a random person stumbling upon it, and most text you find here will likely be incomprehensible.
Then again, I recognize the value a personal website/blog can unintentionally provide to the most disconnected of visitors. Many a time have I caught myself reading through
the entirety of someone’s blog, parasocially investing myself in their life, and maybe picking up a few facts or interests along the way. So while this website is intended
for me to look back on, anyone else who somehow finds themselves consuming most of the content of this website will likely learn a lot about me too.

About me
mi jan Harrison. mi nimi “lythieme” anu “lythienne” kin. I go to Stanford University.
Find me at [my SUNet in the url]@stanford.edu or at my github (linked below).
What do I do next?
Click on any dot in the box to the left, hover over to see the name of the mu (mu). Alternatively, search with the bar to the right. Power-users can even just type a random
english or toki pona word after “…/poki.mu/” in the url and pray I’ve written a mu titled that.

information.title: information
information.content:
text is so fucking long like when i see some of the mus on here [aside: i should write more in toki pona i think this is the third like pure english thing ive written in a row
i want to get to the point where i can just mu off the dome in toki pona]
see how much visual space that aside takes up on the page like i know it would be the same amount of words if i said all that to you in conversation but it feels like so much
information than the amount of visual space the equivalent words take up ykwim. thats my point written language is so inefficient it takes so much more processing power, filler words
end up increasing processing time instead of decreasing it as in spoken speech.
surely there is a better visual method of information transfer → like drawings but those take too long to produce like what is a human optimized to recognize .. faces?
i will continue this later

name.title: name
name.content:
im quite proud of the name of this website i think i was pondering 2 toki pona word combos that were unique but somewhat profound
(after Lipamanka)
→ The semantic space of poki includes anything that is a container (the semantic space of containing involves being a thing that groups many other things together,
separating them from even more other things).
→ The semantic space of mu includes all noises coming from “living” things. mu are implied to be not speech and therefore unintelligible. They do still come from
living things though and are (somewhat) intentional.
isnt that kind of beautiful
im not communicating because there is no one on the other side reading this. but im also not just subconsciously vocal stimming, im just mu-ing into the endless text-slop
filled digital aether

sama.title: sama (by jan mokupona)
sama.content:
suli la mi toki e ijo tan toki [INLI]. LOL
tenpo pini la mama mi li lon e jan tu. tan ni la wan li mi. ante li jan [SUWAN].
jan [SUWAN] li pana e pilin pona. taso tenpo lili la ona li pana e jaki kin.
ni la lon tenpo poka la ona li kama toki e nimi te sama to.
sina toki e nimi te sama to la sina awen pona. taso sina toki e nimi te sama to lon tenpo mute lon tenpo ale la o pakala tawa sina.
nimi te sama to li jo e nasin toki pona wan taso. o ilo e nimi ni tawa ijo taso. ni la toki te sama tomo tawa to anu te sama tomo tawa jelo to anu te sama tomo tawa jelo leko pi jan lili mute to li pona.
sina tawa tan lawa ni la sina pakala. sina ken pakala. sina pakala la sina jan. taso toki te sama sama sama nn sama to anu te sama tu ale ale to anu te sina sama noka e ijo to anu te sina sama lape e ilo to li jaki mute.
ni la jan [SUWAN] li ilo ike e nimi te sama to la mi wile misikeke e ona.
ona toki e nimi te sama to kepeken nasin ike la mi pana e kalama suli pi ilo [WAJEN]. kalama ni li sama kalama pi wawa walo sewi.
mi wile pali e ni lon tenpo sona kulupu mi kin. mi kute e nimi ni pi nasin ike lon tenpo mute la mi wile jaki tan uta li wile moli e mi. taso mi mu ala tan ni: mi wile ala e jan pi pilin ike tawa mi. jan [SUWAN] en mi la ni li musi. taso lon tenpo ante pi jan ante la pali ni li ken pakala. o ilo pona e nimi sina. ni li ale.

member of (have a personal website) ~> member of (into systems cs ^ hate ai)
and also
member of (have a personal website) ⇒ member of (self centered)
and also
member of (speak toki pona) ~> member of (have a personal website)
*where ~> is likely implies and ⇒ is implies 💀
this means people who speak toki pona are likely self centered
